headline: Stockyards district lost as horde crosses the rail yards
detail: Freight cars stand open and empty along the line. The cattle pens are full of things that no longer low.

headline: Forest ranger station reports knocking on every wall
detail: The sound moved clockwise around the cabin for hours. By morning the ranger's truck was gone but he was not.

headline: Speedway grounds overrun during empty practice run
detail: The track loudspeakers carry only a wet rasping now. A lone car circles endlessly with no driver visible.

headline: Subway tunnels evacuated as platforms fill from below
detail: Trains stopped responding at Park Street station. Passengers describe figures walking out of the dark tracks.

headline: Steel district watchman reports clanging from empty mills
detail: The furnaces have been cold for years. Yet something keeps striking the metal in slow, deliberate rhythm.

headline: Skyway system sealed with movement on every level
detail: The enclosed walkways trapped commuters between floors. Glass tunnels now writhe with pale, pressing hands.

headline: The Strip goes dark as the dead flood the boulevard
detail: Every marquee blinked out at once just after midnight. A tide of bodies now moves between the silent towers.

headline: University labs report specimens missing from freezers
detail: The storage room locks were forced from the inside. Wet footprints lead down an empty corridor.

headline: Lighthouse keeper signals SOS then goes dark
detail: The beam swept inland three times against regulation. No one answers the keeper's line.

headline: Mile-high stadium lockdown breached during overtime
detail: The crowd noise changed pitch in an instant. Tens of thousands are now pressing toward the gates as one.

headline: River bridges close as figures climb from the water
detail: Dripping shapes pulled themselves over the rails at the Three Rivers point. Traffic is being turned back.

headline: Cruise ship denied port as moans echo from decks
detail: Harbor pilots report passengers lining the rails in silence. The vessel keeps drifting toward Waikiki.

headline: Historic cemetery gates found torn from hinges
detail: Fresh soil is overturned across the older sections. Caretakers refuse to enter after dark.

headline: Stockyard reports unexplained nighttime stampede
detail: Something herded the cattle into a single trembling mass. The night watchman has not been located.

headline: Border crossing seals as both lanes fill with the dead
detail: Traffic cameras show no living drivers in the stalled lines. The barrier arms keep rising and falling alone.

headline: Lake-effect fog brings drifting shapes to campus
detail: Students describe cold hands tapping dorm windows from the third floor. The fog has not lifted in two days.

headline: Art district mannequins reported rearranging themselves
detail: Shopkeepers swear the figures move between glances. One gallery owner has stopped coming in.

headline: Church bells ring all night with no one inside
detail: The doors were locked at evening service. By dawn the pews were full and the congregation would not leave.

headline: Refinery district burns as the infected ignore flames
detail: Workers report figures walking through the fire unharmed. The smoke smells of something other than oil.

headline: State capitol grounds fill with motionless crowds
detail: They arrived from every direction overnight. None responded to the morning loudspeaker announcements.

headline: Radio station broadcasts looping distress message
detail: The same DJ has repeated one sentence for six hours. Behind his voice, a chair keeps scraping.

headline: Inner Harbor cordon collapses under the crowd
detail: The aquarium glass cracked from the pressure inside. The water has gone dark and the corridors are full.

headline: Blizzard reveals frozen figures that begin to move
detail: Plows uncovered bodies along Highway 29 that should not stand. They are now walking against the wind.

headline: Diner customers freeze mid-meal at sunrise
detail: Coffee grows cold in front of unmoving patrons. The cook says they have not spoken in hours.

headline: Port cranes operate with no one at the controls
detail: Containers are being stacked in strange spiraling patterns. Dockworkers fled before midnight.

headline: Horse farm reports stable hands acting feral
detail: The animals refuse to enter the barn. A low growling comes from the hayloft at dusk.

headline: Casino floor erupts as the dead deal themselves in
detail: Slot machines kept ringing long after the screaming stopped. The cashier cages are smeared red.

headline: Insurance towers go dark as power grid fails
detail: Backup generators failed to engage downtown. Office windows now glow only with pale, drifting faces.

headline: Highway patrol finds abandoned cruiser, doors ajar
detail: The dashcam recorded a deputy walking calmly into the dark fields. He never radioed back.

headline: Honky-tonk strip falls silent for the first time
detail: The neon still burns over Broadway, but no music plays. Slow figures sway in time to nothing.

headline: Light rail trains arriving empty but locked
detail: Each car shows signs of struggle but holds no passengers. The intercom plays only static and chewing.

headline: Convention center quarantine breached overnight
detail: Ten thousand attendees were sealed in for observation. The doors are now opening from the inside.

headline: Cornfield maze traps families with something inside
detail: The screaming came from the center of the labyrinth. The exit gate has since been chained from within.

headline: Beach evacuation fails as infected emerge from surf
detail: Bloated figures walked out of the Gulf at sunset. Lifeguards abandoned their towers within minutes.

headline: Coal town hears moaning from a sealed mine shaft
detail: The old number-six tunnel was closed decades ago. Something has begun knocking from inside.

headline: Cannery night crew reported missing in the dark
detail: Under the long winter night, the docks have gone quiet. Frozen handprints mark the loading bay doors.

headline: Dust storm masks horde advancing on the interstate
detail: Visibility dropped to zero just as the shapes appeared. Drivers are advised not to roll down their windows.

headline: College campus reports students sleepwalking at noon
detail: They gather on the quad facing the same direction. None of them blink.

headline: Mountain trailhead closed after hikers return changed
detail: A party of four came down the ridge moving wrong and silent. Rangers have not been heard from since.

headline: Grain elevator workers vanish during shift change
detail: Only their hard hats remain by the conveyor. The silo continues to fill with something that is not wheat.

headline: Ferry returns to dock with no living crew
detail: The vessel drifted in on its own engines. Passengers describe scratching from below the waterline.

headline: Truck stop attendant locks himself in cooler
detail: He reports a customer who paid, then stood motionless for an hour. The fuel pumps are still running unattended.

headline: Mardi Gras crowd turns in the French Quarter
detail: Masks made it impossible to tell the living from the dead. The beads are still rattling in the gutters.

headline: Lakefront jogger bitten, refuses treatment
detail: He insisted it was just a dog before walking into the trees. He has not come back out.

headline: Ski lodge guests barricaded above the snowline
detail: The infected do not seem slowed by the cold. Boot prints circle the lodge in widening rings.

headline: Freeway interchange becomes feeding ground
detail: Stalled traffic on I-75 left thousands trapped in their cars. The horns have stopped one by one through the night.

headline: Rancher reports cattle fleeing toward town
detail: The herd broke through three fences before dawn. Whatever spooked them left no tracks, only a smell of rot.

headline: Historic district overrun as fog rolls in off the marsh
detail: The cobblestone squares are full of slow-moving silhouettes. Spanish moss hides them until they are already close.

headline: Hospital ward sealed after night-shift incident
detail: Three staff failed to clock out, and something keeps pressing against the maternity glass. Security has stopped answering radios.

headline: Lone shambler spotted near the old paper mill
detail: A single figure dragged itself along the riverbank at dawn. Locals report a low, wet moaning from the treeline.
